title: Dudael — The Quarantine Zone
summary: A sealed region of space in Echo and Void's sector, built to contain fallen angels as forensic evidence, not as a conventional prison.

title: Echo and Void's Sector
summary: The territory of Echo and Void, where Dudael hangs like a secured exhibit—watched, maintained, never opened casually.

title: The Spiral
summary: A grayscale gradient spiral on the title screen, doubling as the path down; read as Light-to-Dark or Dark-to-Light depending on player perspective.

title: The Drop
summary: The moment a run ends, when the fallen are forced to reckon with what they chose—or what was done to them—converting failure into Memory Fragments.

title: Oikētērion
summary: “Proper dwelling” or spiritual habitation; the station the angels abandoned, making them subject to Dudael's containment.

title: The Watchers
summary: Rogue First Engineers who broke from their original function, their transgression echoing in the design of Solo descent and Dark relics.

title: Relics (Dark Cards)
summary: Original artifacts from before the fall, now expressed as Dark cards that bend, conceal, or weaponize the rules of the descent.

title: Radiant Seven Meta-Universe
summary: A larger ecosystem where Dudael is one node; events in the Drop reverberate outward and are not self-contained.

title: Seraph
tagline: Once all Light, now blinded by fragments.
summary: Highest-order angel whose proximity to Light was total before the fall; now navigates Dudael through partial blindness and memory of clarity.

title: Shadow
summary: Defines the fall as disappearance rather than descent, moving through Dudael by absence, concealment, and misdirection.

title: Exile
summary: Cast out instead of choosing to fall, carrying the weight of a sentence they did not select and a zone they did not seek.

title: Penitent
summary: Seeks understanding or atonement, drawn to codex entries and lore; gains strength by engaging with what Dudael reveals.

title: Rebel
summary: Fell by choice, fully aware of the cost; embodies agency and instability, treating Dudael as a system to break rather than endure.

title: The Light Guide — Surveyor
summary: Child aligned with Light, tending an economy of clarity and revelation in the Staging Area and Draft.

title: The Dark Guide — Smuggler
summary: Child aligned with Dark, managing concealment, side deals, and relic trades behind the draft.

title: Staging Keepers
summary: Surveyor and Smuggler maintain the locker room between runs, tracking what the runner reads, touches, and ignores.

title: TITLE — The Void
summary: Entry chamber where the spiral appears and the house is already awake—systems live before the first click.

title: CHARACTER SELECT — The Casting Hall
summary: Gallery of Sigils and profiles where the player first chooses what they inherit, then how they will carry it.

title: STAGING — Locker Room
summary: Quiet room between choosing and acting; initially empty, later filled with run history, unlocked lore, and vessel progression.

title: CARD DRAFT — The Children's Table
summary: Light and Dark guides offer cards; choices here silently construct the next level and tilt the Light/Dark scale.

title: LEVEL PLAY — The Cartridge
summary: A neutral container that mounts different mini-games or puzzles, shaped by prior draft and staging behavior.

title: DOOR — Divergence
summary: Three exits—Light, Dark, Secret—priced in parity and depth, where earlier choices are finally tallied.

title: DROP — The Fall Event
summary: End-of-run chamber where death is logged, Memory Fragments are minted, and the next descent is quietly prepared.

title: Light
summary: Axis of clarity, revelation, and structure; a resource and a record of choices that exposed more than they hid.

title: Dark
summary: Axis of concealment, depth, and transformation; tracks choices that hid, bent, or submerged truth.

title: Secret / Threshold
summary: The synthesis state between Light and Dark, represented in doors and Sinerine's third color pole.

title: Parity
summary: The balance of Light and Dark across the run; snapshot after draft, checked at the Door, and amplified by depth.

title: Memory Fragments
summary: Shards of remembered descent earned on death, spent to change what waits in Staging on future runs.

title: The Loop
summary: The cycle Title → Select → Staging → Draft → Level → Door → Drop, where death feeds progression instead of erasing it.

title: Locker Room Accretion
summary: With each run, more history, codex entries, and vessel changes accumulate in Staging, turning a pass-through into a hub.

title: Forensic Theophany
summary: The mood of Dudael—clinical, examining, evidence under glass; the game presents and the player decides what it means.

title: Sinerine
summary: The design language of Dudael—tri-polar palette, formal typography, and strict tokens that make the UI feel like a controlled experiment.
